symptoms.symptom_name: Prolonged high fever (>39 C) for 6 days, antipyretic-refractory

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fever >=5 days is the mandatory diagnostic criterion for Kawasaki disease.

symptoms.symptom_name: Non-exudative conjunctivitis, cracked erythematous lips, strawberry tongue

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mucocutaneous changes are principal Kawasaki criteria.
Non-exudative conjunctivitis is not typical of scarlet fever.

symptoms.symptom_name: Polymorphous trunk rash with erythema/oedema of hands and feet

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rash and extremity changes are principal Kawasaki criteria.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden lethargy and chest-clutching episode (possible acute cardiac involvement)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: New lethargy and chest pain signal possible myocarditis or coronary ischaemia - an emergency.

symptoms.symptom_name: Painful unilateral right cervical lymphadenopathy (~2 cm)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Cervical lymphadenopathy is a principal Kawasaki criterion.

symptoms.symptom_name: No cough/coryza/Koplik spots; fully vaccinated (against measles)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of cough/coryza/Koplik spots and full vaccination argue against measles.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tachycardia out of proportion to fever and a new systolic murmur

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Disproportionate tachycardia and a new murmur suggest myocarditis/cardiac involvement.

symptoms.symptom_name: Coronary artery aneurysms and impaired function on echocardiography

investigation_details.expected_result: Coronary artery dilatation/aneurysms and/or myocardial dysfunction consistent with Kawasaki cardiac involvement
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Coronary involvement is not detected and treatment/monitoring cannot be tailored.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Coronary aneurysms on echocardiography confirm cardiac involvement in Kawasaki disease.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Complete Kawasaki disease with suspected acute cardiac involvement
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Fever >5 days plus the full mucocutaneous/extremity tetrad and cervical lymphadenopathy, now with lethargy and a chest-clutching episode, establish complete Kawasaki disease with red flags for myocarditis/coronary involvement - a paediatric emergency requiring immediate IVIG and echocardiography.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assesses coronary arteries (aneurysms) and cardiac function; performed urgently as an inpatient.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Inflammatory markers, FBC and supportive bloods
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Support the diagnosis and monitor disease activity/complications.
Detects ischaemic changes/arrhythmia from cardiac involvement.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Continued home antipyretics with outpatient review
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Delays time-critical IVIG in a child with possible cardiac involvement; immediate transfer is required.
